details:
Follow-up localizing the fleet-wide native gameplay-CI red (see #1227,
#1260). Investigated the failing gameplay tests seen across recent native
PR runs (e.g. PR #1252 linux job, base 4451816 which includes #1249):

  [test FAIL 3.492s] test.GameTest.test_map_walkthrough_ninemarches
  [test FAIL 0.724s] test.GameTest.test_map_walkthrough_sunderedmarch
  [test FAIL 43.1s ] test.McpServerTest.test_stdio_map_walkthrough_ninemarches
  [test FAIL 0.022s] test.GameTest.test_player_progression_through_level_six_unlocks_once_per_class
  + shards abort with SIGABRT ("terminate called without an active exception", exit -6)
  + one shard times out after 1109s (exit 124)

Key finding: commit a4ccfee "Fix player entry placed on self-removing
StartEvent (ninemarches, sunderedmarch) (#1249)" was a TARGETED FIX for the
ninemarches/sunderedmarch walkthrough failures. It moved the player entry
two tiles off the self-removing StartEvent (map.json, 2 lines each) and
added a validate_content.py guard. Verified via git:
  - a4ccfee is on origin/main and is an ancestor of BOTH PR bases 4451816
    and 0db599f, so those PRs' CI ran WITH the #1249 fix applied.
  - Yet test_map_walkthrough_ninemarches / _sunderedmarch STILL FAIL in
    those post-#1249 runs, and fail FAST (3.5s / 0.7s) -- i.e. a real
    assertion failure, not the huge-map shard timeout that previously
    masked them (the #1249 message itself notes the failure was masked by
    the timeout before).
  => #1249 did NOT close the ninemarches/sunderedmarch walkthrough
     failures. Either the entry move is insufficient, or (note) #1249
     changed map.json entry tiles but did NOT update the walkthrough test
     route, so the scripted GameTest walkthrough may now diverge from the
     relocated entry. Needs a build-capable follow-up to read the actual
     current assertion (per-test output was not in the retained CI tail;
     this environment cannot build the _game extension to reproduce).

Impact: linux/windows native jobs are red on every PR via this shared
gameplay suite, so per-PR native signal is unusable (e.g. #1261's linux
and windows failures are this, not the PR's own change). The four adopted
archetype PRs and the P0 CI-authority work were gated on fast-path +
own-test evidence for exactly this reason.

evidence.note: base includes a4ccfee (#1249) per git merge-base --is-ancestor

evidence.crash: SIGABRT terminate called without an active exception; shards exit -6; one shard exit 124 after 1109s

evidence.priorFix: a4ccfee #1249 moved entry 2 tiles off StartEvent in ninemarches/sunderedmarch map.json + validate_content.py guard; did NOT touch the walkthrough test route

impact: Native linux/windows red on every PR via the shared gameplay suite; per-PR native signal unusable (e.g. #1261). A prior targeted fix (#1249) is confirmed insufficient, so the gate needs build-capable diagnosis of the current ninemarches/sunderedmarch assertion + the SIGABRT + huge-map shard timeout
suggestedImprovement: Build-capable follow-up: capture the current per-test assertion for the ninemarches/sunderedmarch walkthroughs (no longer masked by timeout post-#1249); check whether #1249's entry relocation left the scripted walkthrough route stale; root-cause the SIGABRT and the huge-map shard 1109s timeout separately
summary: #1249's targeted fix did NOT resolve ninemarches/sunderedmarch walkthroughs: they still fail fast (assertion, not timeout) in post-#1249 CI; gameplay suite keeps native CI red fleet-wide
